# tags: #hashtags-primary + explicit; filter; rm-guard; edit-delta reconciliation
mkdir proj
cd proj
exec mtt init
stdout 'initialized'

# add: a #hashtag in the title + an explicit --tag; both land, canonical (sorted)
exec mtt add --type epic 'fix #auth' --tag urgent
stdout 'created e1'
exec mtt show e1
stdout 'tags:     auth, urgent'

# a Cyrillic hashtag works end-to-end (Unicode charset)
exec mtt add --type epic 'чиним #бэкенд'
stdout 'created e2'
exec mtt show e2
stdout 'tags:     бэкенд'
exec mtt list --tag бэкенд
stdout 'e2  epic  \[tbd\]'
! stdout 'e1'

# tag add (variadic) then tag rm on a manual tag
exec mtt tag add e1 backend deploy
stdout 'tagged e1: backend, deploy'
exec mtt show e1
stdout 'tags:     auth, backend, deploy, urgent'
exec mtt tag rm e1 urgent
stdout 'untagged e1: urgent'

# GUARD: cannot rm a text-anchored tag while #auth is in the title
! exec mtt tag rm e1 auth
stderr 'cannot remove tag "auth": #auth is present in the title'

# filter: --tag auth matches e1, not e2
exec mtt list --tag auth
stdout 'e1  epic  \[tbd\]'
! stdout 'e2'

# tree --tag filters the hierarchy too (keep-ancestors over the same dimension)
exec mtt tree --tag backend
stdout 'e1  epic  \[tbd\]'
! stdout 'e2'

# edit the title to drop #auth -> the auth tag disappears (delta reconciliation)
exec mtt edit e1 --title 'fix login'
exec mtt show e1
! stdout 'auth'
exec mtt list --tag auth
! stdout 'e1'

# an invalid tag is a usage error
! exec mtt tag add e1 'bad tag'
stderr 'invalid tag'

# a missing id is not-found (non-zero exit; the exact exit-4 mapping is a unit test)
! exec mtt tag add ghost x
stderr 'not found'

# --json carries the tags array
exec mtt show e1 --json
stdout '"tags"'

# --exclude-tag: hide tasks carrying a tag, on list AND ready (de-noise the queue)
exec mtt add --type epic 'noise' --tag backlog
stdout 'created e3'
exec mtt list
stdout 'e3  epic'
# --exclude-tag backlog hides e3, keeps the others
exec mtt list --exclude-tag backlog
! stdout 'e3'
stdout 'e1'
# exclude wins over include when both name the same tag
exec mtt list --tag backlog --exclude-tag backlog
! stdout 'e3'
# ready honors --exclude-tag too
exec mtt ready
stdout 'e3  epic'
exec mtt ready --exclude-tag backlog
! stdout 'e3'
stdout 'e1'

# mtt tags: the tag vocabulary with per-tag counts (default open scope) + --json
exec mtt tag add e2 backend
exec mtt tags
stdout '2  backend'
stdout '1  backlog'
exec mtt tags --json
stdout '"tag"'
stdout '"count"'
# the list filters compose: --exclude-tag backlog drops e3, so backlog isn't counted
exec mtt tags --exclude-tag backlog
! stdout 'backlog'
stdout 'backend'
